name: generate_document
description:
Generate or replace a professional Office document (DOCX, XLSX, or PPTX) at the specified path within a registered source directory. Paths may be absolute or relative to a source root.

Use this tool when the user asks to create or update a Word document, Excel spreadsheet, or PowerPoint presentation. Think through audience, tone, hierarchy, and layout before calling it.

Content format varies by document type:
- DOCX: supports `title`, `subtitle`, `cover_note`, optional top-level `theme`, and `sections`.
  Each section can include `heading`, `body`, `bullet_items`, `callout`, `table`, and `page_break_before`.
  `callout` = highlighted box with `{ "title": "...", "body": "...", "tone": "info|warning|success" }`.
  `table` = `{ "title": "...", "headers": [...], "rows": [[...], ...], "column_widths": [2,1,1] }` where `column_widths` are relative weights.
- XLSX: each sheet supports `name`, optional `title`, `notes`, `headers`, `rows`, `column_widths`, `tab_color`, `freeze_rows`, and `autofilter`.
  Rows may contain primitive cells or styled objects like `{ "value": 1200, "num_format": "$#,##0", "bold": true, "align": "right", "background_color": "EAF3FB" }` or `{ "formula": "=SUM(B2:B8)", "bold": true }`.
- PPTX: supports optional top-level `theme` plus slide layouts `title`, `content`, `section`, and `comparison`.
  Slides may include `title`, `subtitle`, `body`, `bullet_items`; comparison slides may additionally include `left_title`, `left_body`, `left_bullet_items`, `right_title`, `right_body`, and `right_bullet_items`.

Prefer elegant structure over dumping raw text. Use slides, sheets, and sections intentionally.

path.description: Output file path (absolute or relative to a source directory). Must be within a registered source directory.

content.description: Structured content for the document. Schema depends on the format chosen.
